Metadata.title: Astra OS — AI Operating System | Private Alpha
Metadata.description: Linux-based AI OS with kernel-level agent orchestration. Limited slots available for the Private Alpha.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: Astra OS collects only the data you voluntarily provide during waitlist signup. We do not sell, rent, or share your data with third parties.
Privacy.dataCollectedItems:
First name, last name, and email address (required)
Answers to 4 qualifying questions (role, AI agent usage, interests, motivation)
Browser locale and referral source (analytics only, not linked to your identity)
Privacy.retentionBody: Your data is retained for the duration of the closed-beta period. You may request deletion at any time by emailing the address below.
Privacy.rightsBody: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. For KVKK rights (Turkish residents), the same rights apply under Turkish data protection law.
Privacy.contactBody: For any privacy-related requests, email: privacy@astraos.com

Hero.headline: Your OS doesn't know what an AI agent is. Astra does.
Hero.subheadline: Astra OS is a Linux-based operating system with kernel-level AI agent orchestration — built for developers who are serious about what comes next.
Hero.scarcity: Limited slots available for the Private Alpha.

Features.sectionTitle: Built differently, from the ground up.
Features.card1Body: Built on a hardened Linux kernel — not a wrapper, not a compatibility layer. The same proven foundation that runs the world's most critical infrastructure, now optimized for AI workloads.
Features.card2Body: Every line of Astra OS that can be open will be. No black boxes, no lock-in. You can inspect the scheduler, audit the agent runtime, and fork whatever you need.
Features.card3Body: AI agent scheduling happens at the kernel, not in userspace middleware. Agents get real process isolation, direct hardware access, and first-class resource guarantees.
Features.card4Body: Agents never start from zero. A high-speed persistent memory layer preserves context across sessions, processes, and reboots — at kernel speed, not application latency.
Features.card5Body: A priority-aware scheduler gives AI agents the CPU time they need, when they need it. No competing with background services for an unmanaged thread pool.
Features.card6Body: A first-class runtime for generative applications that adapt, learn, and execute on-device. Build apps that reason — not just apps that run.

WhyAstra.para1: General-purpose operating systems were built for humans clicking buttons — not for autonomous agents scaling logic. Their kernel schedulers have no concept of reasoning load, context windows, or inter-agent urgency. When your agent gets throttled, loses context mid-chain, or crashes under memory pressure, you are not fighting your code. You are fighting an OS designed for the pre-AI era.
WhyAstra.para2: Layering AI frameworks on top of a legacy OS gets you further — but the foundation stays broken. Agent orchestration ends up in Python libraries. Process isolation is bolted on. Every agent run competes with background services for an unmanaged thread pool the OS was never designed to share intelligently. The latency is not a bug in your prompt — it is architectural.
WhyAstra.para3: Astra OS starts from a different question: what if the OS knew agents were the primary workload? Kernel-level scheduling priorities for agent processes. First-class inter-agent communication via IPC primitives, not HTTP. Resource quotas enforced at the scheduler, not the application layer. Persistent memory that grows with your workflow — so agents never start from zero. This is not a framework — it is the operating system itself.

FinalCTA.headline: Stop fighting your OS. Let your agents thrive.
FinalCTA.scarcity: Join the Private Alpha. Only 100 early access slots remaining.

Footer.description: The AI Operating System for the next era of autonomous software.
